public class PIDController {
    public double calculate(double setpoint, double measurement) {
        double error = setpoint - measurement; // Allocates double
        double integral = integral + error; // Allocates double
        double derivative = error - previousError; // Allocates double
        previousError = error; // Allocates double

}

public class PIDController {
    private double integral = 0.0;
    private double previousError = 0.0;

    public double calculate(double setpoint, double measurement) {
        double error = setpoint - measurement; // Stack allocation
        integral += error; // Reuses field
        double derivative = error - previousError; // Stack allocation
        previousError = error; // Reuses field
        return Kp * error + Ki * integral + Kd * derivative; // No allocations
    }
}

Every year, hundreds of teams attempt to copy-paste their advanced time-of-flight models or trajectory solvers. And every year, their robots break due to architectural bloat and corrupted state variables."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"In ARESLib, we do not copy-paste. We perform structurally rigid "},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"structure Mining"},{"type":"text","text":" governed by our AI Agentic Skills framework."}]},{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":2},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"1 The Abstraction Integrity Rule"}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"In 2024, Team 1690 released an incredible time-of-flight interpolation engine for shooting on the move. However, their repository was monolithicΓÇöa beautiful mess of interwoven singletons."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"When mining this logic, you must strictly strip the target repository of its structure and salvage "},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"italic"}],"text":"only the raw mathematics"},{"type":"text","text":". If the original code calls a motor controller directly inside the math solver, you must replace it with a decoupled "},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"code"}],"text":"ARESMechanismTarget"},{"type":"text","text":" payload."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":":::caution "},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"AI Agent Enforcement"}]},{"type":"codeBlock","attrs":{"language":null},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"ARESLib natively enforces this boundary via the `marslib-elite-mining` skill. Lead engineers can command the AI to ingest a target repository file, extract the discrete state-space models, and map them purely into our AdvantageKit `IO` layer pattern. The AI is forbidden from ingesting the hardware implementations of rival teams.
"}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":":::"}]},{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":2},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"2 Telemetry Rewiring"}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Elite teams often use complex Dashboard configurations or raw "},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"code"}],"text":"SmartDashboard"},{"type":"text","text":" calls. When bringing this logic into ARESLib, all telemetry calls must be gutted and replaced explicitly with AdvantageKit "},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"code"}],"text":"Logger.recordOutput(...)"},{"type":"text","text":" arrays, preserving determinism."}]},{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":2},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"3 Mathematical Normalization"}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Team 254 uses custom "},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"code"}],"text":"Twist2d"},{"type":"text","text":" math. Team 2910 uses "},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"code"}],"text":"ChassisSpeeds"},{"type":"text","text":". When ingesting kinematics, you must normalize the source mathematics into ARESLib''s expected physical units utilizing standard "},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"link","attrs":{"href":"https://docs.wpilib.org/en/stable/docs/software/advanced-controls/geometry/index.html","target":"_blank","rel":"noopener noreferrer nofollow","class":null,"title":null}}],"text":"ARESLib / FTClib geometry structs"},{"type":"text","text":" (e.g. "},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"code"}],"text":"Translation2d"},{"type":"text","text":" in Meters, not Inches; Radians, not Degrees). No raw sensor ticks are allowed past the IO layer interface."}]},{"type":"horizontalRule"},{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":2},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"≡ƒôû Further Reading & External Resources"}]},{"type":"codeBlock","attrs":{"language":null},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"- [ARESLib / FTClib Official Documentation](https://docs.wpilib.org/en/stable/) - The definitive baseline resource.

- [1690 Code Release (Chief Delphi)](https://www.chiefdelphi.com/t/team-1690-orbit-2024-code-release/464817) - Case study in advanced trajectory interpolation.
"}]}]}

In ARESLib, these values aren''t just posters on the wall; they are the architectural principles that govern our code, our collaboration, and our impact on the FTC community."}]},{"type":"interactiveComponent","attrs":{"componentName":"HomeCoreValues"}},{"type":"horizontalRule"},{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":2},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"0 Why Culture Matters in Code"}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Elite robotics software is more than just math and logic. ARESLib was designed to be a platform where:"}]},{"type":"bulletList","content":[{"type":"listItem","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Innovation"},{"type":"text","text":" is encouraged by making simulation accessible to every student."}]}]},{"type":"listItem","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Inclusion"},{"type":"text","text":" is enforced by building world-class accessibility into our documentation."}]}]},{"type":"listItem","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Impact"},{"type":"text","text":" is measured by how much we help other teams grow through our open-source contributions."}]}]}]},{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":2},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Our Philosophy"}]},{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":3},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Discovery & Innovation"}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"We explore new skills and ideas with creative persistence. ARESLib''s "},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"zero-allocation"},{"type":"text","text":" and "},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"250Hz odometry"},{"type":"text","text":" are the results of relentless innovationΓÇöpushing beyond standard solutions to find what works best in a championship environment."}]},{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":3},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Teamwork & Inclusion"}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"We are stronger when we work together and respect our differences. In honor of our founding mentor Phil Tucker and the Tucker Teams initiative, we live by the motto: "},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"\"No robot left behind.\""}]},{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":3},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Impact & Fun"}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"We use what we learn to improve our world and celebrate our successes! Every "},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"code"}],"text":"BUILD SUCCESSFUL"},{"type":"text","text":" is a moment for "},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Fun"},{"type":"text","text":". We share our work so that the entire FTC community can benefit from our discovery. As the 2017 FIRST Championship Chairman''s (Impact) Award winners, we ensure our code structure embodies \"The ARES Plan\"ΓÇöacting as a catalyst to Engage, Inspire, and Sustain the next generation of innovators."}]},{"type":"horizontalRule"},{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":2},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"ARESLib Specific Values"}]},{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":3},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"The Appalachian Mindset"}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"FTC engineering is inherently difficult. We actively encourage students to cultivate grit and determination when learning hard things. Transitioning to zero-allocation loops, advanced odometry, and high-fidelity physics requires immense persistence. The Appalachian Mindset means pushing through the initial learning curve, pursuing non-stop innovation, and choosing the complete, mathematically robust path even when it is challenging."}]},{"type":"horizontalRule"},{"type":"blockquote","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"\"The robots are the vehicle; the students are the cargo.\" ΓÇö "},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Team ARES 23247"}]}]}]}

results.title: The Appalachian Mindset
results.content: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"At "},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"ARES 23247"},{"type":"text","text":", we recognize that creating world-class robots doesn''t happen by taking the easy way out. That is why "},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"The Appalachian Mindset"},{"type":"text","text":" is a foundational pillar of our team''s ethos and the ARESLib framework. It is defined by three core traits: "},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Grit"},{"type":"text","text":", "},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Determination"},{"type":"text","text":", and "},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Innovation"},{"type":"text","text":"."}]},{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":2},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Grit in the Face of Complexity"}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"FTC software engineering is inherently difficult. You will face concepts that feel insurmountable:"}]},{"type":"bulletList","content":[{"type":"listItem","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Transitioning from simple "},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"code"}],"text":"TimedRobot"},{"type":"text","text":" architectures to advanced "},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"State Machines"},{"type":"text","text":"."}]}]},{"type":"listItem","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Trusting "},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Zero-Mocking"},{"type":"text","text":" simulations to validate logic before the robot is built."}]}]},{"type":"listItem","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Adopting "},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Zero-Allocation"},{"type":"text","text":" logic rather than continually relying on the garbage collector."}]}]}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"It takes immense grit to learn that the \"easy way\" will ultimately bottleneck the robot in competition. The Appalachian Mindset means pushing through the initial learning curve and choosing the mathematically robust pathΓÇöeven when it requires hours of reading documentation and tuning SysId feedforwards."}]},{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":2},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Determination to Fail Forward"}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"We encourage students to actively engage with failure as a tool for progress:"}]},{"type":"orderedList","attrs":{"start":1,"type":null},"content":[{"type":"listItem","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Take on Hard Tickets:"},{"type":"text","text":" Volunteer for the subsystem you don''t instantly understand. Have the determination to figure it out."}]}]},{"type":"listItem","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Fail Forward:"},{"type":"text","text":" When a simulation test crashes or the robot behaves erratically, do not retreat to old code. Analyze the AdvantageKit log, diagnose the root cause, and formulate a solution."}]}]},{"type":"listItem","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Persist Through Bugs:"},{"type":"text","text":" Finding the elusive NaN error deep in the Swerve kinematics takes determination. We don''t give up; we dig deeper."}]}]}]},{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":2},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Continuous Innovation"}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Grit and determination ultimately fuel "},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Innovation"},{"type":"text","text":". By refusing to settle for standard solutions and constantly pushing the boundaries of what our software can do, we develop groundbreaking architectures like 250Hz Odometry threads and flawless hardware abstractions."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"By adopting the Appalachian Mindset, we ensure that our students emerge from ARES not just as capable developers, but as resilient, innovative engineers ready to tackle the hardest problems the world has to offer."}]}]}
